prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal peaks with school attendance

incidence.source: Primary care and school health cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; substantial school absenteeism and antibiotic use

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment; rapid antigen test/culture when available to confirm streptococcal infection; evaluate for complications (peritonsillar abscess) and rheumatic fever risk

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for symptom relief; antibiotic stewardship limited by diagnostic access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; confirmatory testing availability varies widely
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral; antibiotics for suspected/confirmed streptococcal disease (penicillin/amoxicillin); ENT evaluation for recurrent severe disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overuse of antibiotics, limited diagnostic testing in primary care, delayed ENT access in many districts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost effective antibiotics widely available; strong primary care coverage in many regions
